title: Ancient DNA Extraction from Skeletal Material
author: Irina Velsko
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.baksicwe
materials: High Pure Viral Nucleic Acid kit

steps.title: Prepare binding buffer calculating 10 mL, / reaction
steps.instruction: Prepare binding buffer calculating 10 mL, / reaction . %s %s

steps.title: Prepare wash buffer by pipetting 40...
steps.instruction: Prepare wash buffer by pipetting 40 mL ethanol to the wash buffer from the High Pure Viral Nucleic Acid kit following manufacturer's instuctions and make an aliquot calculating 900 µL, / reaction .

steps.title: Irradiate all buffers with UV for 00:30:00 without the lids
steps.instruction: Irradiate all buffers with UV for 00:30:00 without the lids. UV irradiation can be done together with solutions from steps 1 (binding buffer), 4 (wash buffer), and 5 (TET buffer).

steps.title: Seal tubes with Parafilm, rotate Overnight,...
steps.instruction: Seal tubes with Parafilm, rotate Overnight, (12-18h) with low overhead rotation speed (e.g., 12-16 rpm) at 37 °C in the incubator. Post-incubation lysate and pellet can safely be stored in a freezer ( -20 °C ) before isolation and clean-up. After starting the incubation, we recommend beginning preparations for day 2, such as pre-labelling the falcon tubes that will be used in step 12.

steps.title: For each reaction prepare one 50...
steps.instruction: For each reaction prepare one 50 mL Falcon tube, one High Pure Extender Assembly (i.e. Falcon tube from kit containing funnel and purification column), two collection tubes from the kit, and one 1.5 mL LoBind tube for final elution step.

steps.title: In every 50 mL Falcon tube...
steps.instruction: In every 50 mL Falcon tube pipette 10 mL binding buffer and 400 µL sodium acetate (UV-irradiated). Mix by inversion and measure pH (should be 5-6). Add more sodium acetate if the pH is too high. If the pH is too low you can add sodium hydroxide.

steps.title: Remove parafilm from extraction tubes, then...
steps.instruction: Remove parafilm from extraction tubes, then spin the tubes for 00:02:00 at 18500 x g, °C, to pellet bone powder. If pellet is not solid, repeat centrifugation.

steps.title: Pipette supernatant to matching 50 mL...
steps.instruction: Pipette supernatant to matching 50 mL Falcon tube, mix contents by inversion. If pellet is too fragile, repeat centrifugation before transferring supernatant. Store the bone pellet at -20 °C .

steps.title: Pipette binding buffer/extract mix to High Pure Extender Assembly
steps.instruction: Pipette binding buffer/extract mix to High Pure Extender Assembly.

steps.title: Spin at a maximum of 1500 rpm, °C, for 00:08:00
steps.instruction: Spin at a maximum of 1500 rpm, °C, for 00:08:00 . This RPM is specific to a 50 ml Thermo Scientific TX-400 Swinging Bucket Rotor. As this is a swing rotor, the rpm value maybe inconsistent for other models. Therefore this value must be adjusted on a per-machine basis. Convert the rpm to rcf (g) and determine the appropriate rpm for your instrument. You can also turn the tube 180 o after 00:04:00 to ensure the liquid does not get stuck on the inner rim of the funnel During this centrifugation step, we recommend preparing downstream steps, such as labelling of final elution tubes.

steps.title: Pipette any liquids remaining in the funnel onto the column
steps.instruction: Pipette any liquids remaining in the funnel onto the column. Remove funnel from column and insert the column into a fresh collection tube and take off the funnel.

steps.title: Dry spin the column in the...
steps.instruction: Dry spin the column in the collection tube for 00:02:00 at 18500 x g, °C, .

steps.title: Pipette 450 µL wash buffer from...
steps.instruction: Pipette 450 µL wash buffer from the High Pure Viral Nucleic Acid kit and spin at 8000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Remove column from the collection tube,...
steps.instruction: Remove column from the collection tube, discard the flow-through and the old collection tube, and put the column into a fresh collection tube.

steps.title: Repeat washing step once ( go...
steps.instruction: Repeat washing step once ( go to step #19 ) reusing the collection tube, and discard flow-through. Discard flow-through in one of two following ways: Remove all liquid in the collection tube with a pipette, or Pour off the liquid into a fresh waste tube, and pat the rim of the collection tube dry on a paper tissue or towl. Use just one spot on the paper tissue per sample. Be careful not to touch the rim of the tube on the waste container. Be sure to clean the surface with DNA Away or bleach after discarding the paper.

steps.title: Dry spin at 18500 x g,...
steps.instruction: Dry spin at 18500 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 To ensure the liquid does not get stuck on the inner rim of the funnel, you can optionally spin for 00:00:30 , turn the tube 180 o , and dry spin for another 00:00:30

steps.title: Transfer column into 1
steps.instruction: Transfer column into 1.5 mL LoBind Tube, pipette 50 µL of TET to the center of column to elute DNA, incubate for 00:03:00 and spin 00:01:00 at 18500 x g, °C, . Eluted DNA will be stored in this tube. Label tube on top and side accordingly.

steps.title: Repeat elution step for a total...
steps.instruction: Repeat elution step for a total elution volume of 100 µL TET.

steps.title: Store the DNA extracts at -20 °C until use
steps.instruction: Store the DNA extracts at -20 °C until use.

steps.title: Prepare TET elution buffer by making...
steps.instruction: Prepare TET elution buffer by making an aliquot of TE-buffer calculating 100 µL, / reaction and pipette Tween-20 accordingly to reach 0.05 % (v/v) concentration to make 'TET'. Because Tween-20 is highly viscous, we dilute it 1:10 in UV-HPLC water, and use this 10% dilution to add Tween-20 to the TE-buffer

steps.title: Weigh GuHCl and transfer into a glass bottle
steps.instruction: Weigh GuHCl and transfer into a glass bottle. If you want to clean the area where GuHCl was used, first use water and then bleach solution. Do not use bleach directly as it reacts with GuHCl to produce toxic gases!

steps.title: Fill up with UV-irradiated HPLC water to final volume
steps.instruction: Fill up with UV-irradiated HPLC water to final volume. This reaction is endothermic and the tube will become very cold. Be aware of the unusual 'slushy' way of dissolving.

steps.title: Gently shake horizontally in order to get the salt dissolved
steps.instruction: Gently shake horizontally in order to get the salt dissolved. If necessary, apply short ( 00:00:10 ) bursts in microwave (~400W) keeping the tube slightly unscrewed. Wait until bottle cools down between microwave bursts.

steps.title: Pipette isopropanol to reach the final...
steps.instruction: Pipette isopropanol to reach the final reaction volume ( 10 mL ).

steps.title: Prepare extraction buffer calculating to a...
steps.instruction: Prepare extraction buffer calculating to a total of 1 mL, / reaction . %s %s

steps.title: Prepare extraction buffer by following the...
steps.instruction: Prepare extraction buffer by following the table ( go to step #8 ).

steps.title: Make an aliquot of EDTA ( 0
steps.instruction: Make an aliquot of EDTA ( 0.5 Molarity (M) , pH 8.0 ) calculating 0.9 mL, / reaction and irradiate with UV for 00:30:00 . UV irradiation can be done together with solutions from steps 1 (binding buffer), 4 (wash buffer), and 5 (TET buffer).

steps.title: Store binding buffer in a fridge...
steps.instruction: Store binding buffer in a fridge at 4 °C overnight for day 2. Label the bottle accordingly with the name, date and for the calculated amount of reactions. Buffer can be stored in a fridge for up to four weeks. Seal bottle with parafilm to avoid evaporation.

steps.title: Pipette 1 mL of from the...
steps.instruction: Pipette 1 mL of from the extraction buffer aliquot to a 30 mg - 50 mg aliquot of bone powder. Preparation of the bone or tooth powder ideally should be done prior to beginning of the extraction protocol in a dedicated sampling room, and stored in a labelled 2 mL Safe-Lock tube.

steps.title: Prepare cleaned workspace with all necessary reagents and equipment
steps.instruction: Prepare cleaned workspace with all necessary reagents and equipment. If lab-wide large-batch pre-prepared reagent stores are used, ensure to have made personal stock-aliquot of reagents such as UV-Water, EDTA, sodium acetate, and proteinase K in amounts sufficient for this extraction.

steps.title: Prepare cleaned workspace with all necessary reagents and equipment
steps.instruction: Prepare cleaned workspace with all necessary reagents and equipment.

steps.title: Dilute proteinase K powder ( 100...
steps.instruction: Dilute proteinase K powder ( 100 mg ) to a concentration of 10 mg / 1 mL . Remaining proteinase K solution should be stored in -20 °C
